design_philosophy.core_loop: One-on-one fighting matches with health depletion; players execute special moves via button combinations and manage Ki gauge charging for power attacks
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Execution-based combat with special move timing and counterattack mechanics; knowledge of character movesets versus opponent adaptation
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as iconic Dragon Ball Z characters (Goku, Vegeta, Gohan, Cell, Broly) in signature martial arts battles with distinctive special attacks and transformations
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately positions itself as Street Fighter II-style 2D fighter adapted for Dragon Ball franchise; emphasizes character authenticity (film characters included) over mechanical innovation; sequel philosophy focused on incremental improvement (more stages, adventure elements in tournament mode) rather than systemic overhaul

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Game supports selectable difficulty modes (Novice, Adept, NG+ Very Hard visible in speedrun categories); single-player tournament/story modes provide structured difficulty progression

progression.upgrade_system: Character transformations and Ki gauge management system; character roster with individual move sets
progression.unlock_mechanism: Two characters (Goku, Broly) unlockable via cheat code
replayability_hooks: Multiple playable modes (Story, Tournament, and additional modes)
10-character roster with individual movesets and transformations
Character-specific meteor combos for combo execution variety

world_building_techniques: Character selection reflects Dragon Ball Z narrative arc (Cell Games arc focus)
Inclusion of film characters (Broly, Bojack) alongside canon roster signals expanded universe storytelling
Tournament mode framing provides competitive context for fights

faction_indicators: Hero characters (Goku, Gohan, Vegeta redemption arc)
danger_signals: Broly presence signals combat difficulty escalation (legendary super saiyan power fantasy)
Cell as end-game tournament opponent reflects narrative climax
Bojack and film villains indicate expanded threat roster
environmental_storytelling: Water-based arenas (new to sequel) provide thematic battlefield variety
Split-screen radar mechanic maintains spatial narrative consistency during combat

sources_used: wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Super Butōden 2 gameplay section (control scheme, modes, character roster, new features, development philosophy)
speedrun.com: Speedrun categories showing Story Mode, World Tournament, NG+ difficulty tiers
